description: Having knowledge or perception of a situation or fact; conscious. 'Selua' names the quality of knowing presence—sensing what is, perceiving surroundings, consciousness turned toward experience.
sound_symbolism: The word opens to perception. 'Se' begins with sensing attention, awareness reaching. Then 'lu' flows with open receptivity. Finally 'a' awakens fully, perception complete.
grammatical_notes: Functions as an adjective: 'selua miona' (aware person). Related to 'thesua' (mindful) but broader—awareness of anything, not specifically mindful attention.

pillars.secular-buddhist-philosophy: Awareness is the foundation of practice—knowing what is happening while it is happening.
pillars.peace-linguistics-practices: Aware communication notices impact—sensing how words land, perceiving the other's state.
